data.change_summary: Eighteenth remediation round addressing 4 amendments: (1) Added retryable_failed_attempts.retain() in CycleAdvanced handler to clear stale prior-cycle entries, preventing unbounded memory growth across remediation cycles. (2) Improved apply_jitter randomness by adding a murmur-style hash-mixing step (h ^= h >> 16; h = h.wrapping_mul(0x45d9f3b); h ^= h >> 16) before the modulo, ensuring well-distributed jitter even on coarse-clock systems without adding a new dependency. (3) CRITICAL: Fixed SC-RESUME-005 conformance failure by clearing retryable_failed_attempts when a stage emits will_retry=false (terminal failure or retries exhausted). Previously the map only recorded will_retry=true entries but never removed them on terminal failure, causing run resume to restart at the stale counter value instead of attempt 1. Added unit test terminal_failure_clears_retry_tracking validating the fix. (4) Reverted pre-backoff snapshot write from best-effort (if let Err with tracing::warn) back to ? propagation. The stage_failed journal event has already been durably appended before the snapshot write; if the snapshot fails and the process dies during backoff, journal and snapshot diverge into a non-resumable state, which is the exact condition the snapshot is meant to prevent.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel.

The pre-backoff snapshot write now propagates errors via ?, which aborts the retry loop if the write fails. The in-memory snapshot has already been mutated (status=Failed, active_run=None) but the on-disk snapshot remains in the previous Running state. The error propagates to the caller, and the operator sees the write error. This is correct — aborting is better than silently entering a state where journal and snapshot diverge, making the run non-resumable.

The jitter hash-mixing step uses a fixed murmur-style constant (0x45d9f3b). This is not cryptographically random but provides sufficient entropy dispersion for retry staggering. Two processes calling within the same nanosecond would still get identical jitter, but this is vanishingly unlikely in practice, and the mixing ensures coarse-clock granularity (e.g. 1ms resolution) no longer produces identical values for closely-spaced calls.

The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys. Doc comments clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal, but operator logs will still show 'binary_not_found' for missing env vars.

The retryable_failed_attempts.retain() in CycleAdvanced uses >= current_cycle to keep entries for the current and future cycles. In the unlikely event of a journal with out-of-order cycle numbers, entries from a future cycle would be preserved, which is the correct conservative behavior.

steps.description: CRITICAL: Fixed SC-RESUME-005 conformance failure in derive_resume_state StageFailed handler (engine.rs:5836-5845): added an else branch that calls retryable_failed_attempts.remove(&(stage_id, cycle)) when will_retry=false. Terminal failures (non-retryable or retries exhausted) now clear the retry counter so a resumed run starts at attempt 1 instead of the stale counter value + 1.

Reverted pre-backoff snapshot write from best-effort (if let Err with tracing::warn) back to ? propagation at engine.rs:4643-4648. The stage_failed journal event is already durably appended before this point; if the snapshot write fails and the process crashes during backoff, the journal says 'failed/retrying' while run.json is in its old state, producing a non-resumable journal/snapshot divergence. The snapshot must propagate errors to maintain consistency.

Added retryable_failed_attempts.retain(|&(_, c), _| c >= current_cycle) in the CycleAdvanced handler of derive_resume_state (engine.rs:5852), after updating current_cycle. Drops retry history for cycles that are now behind the cursor, preventing unbounded memory growth in runs with many remediation cycles. Matches the pattern used by CompletionRoundAdvanced's .clear().

Improved apply_jitter randomness in retry_policy.rs:134-155: added a murmur-style hash-mixing step before the modulo operation. The raw subsec_nanos value is now run through h ^= h >> 16; h = h.wrapping_mul(0x45d9f3b); h ^= h >> 16 to spread entropy across all bits. On coarse-clock systems (e.g. 1ms resolution VMs), raw nanos % 1000 would repeat identical values; the hash-mixing ensures distinct jitter even with limited clock granularity. No new dependency added — rand crate is not in Cargo.lock.

Added unit test terminal_failure_clears_retry_tracking in engine.rs resume_state_tests module: 4 retryable StageFailed events (will_retry=true) at attempts 1-4, followed by a terminal StageFailed (will_retry=false) at attempt 5. Asserts resume cursor starts at attempt 1, not 6 (stale counter + 1). Directly validates the SC-RESUME-005 conformance fix.

Verified all tests pass: 683 integration tests passed (cargo test --test unit), 341 library tests passed (cargo test --lib, up from 340 with the new terminal_failure_clears_retry_tracking test), cargo clippy clean, cargo fmt --check clean.

validation_evidence: cargo test --test unit: 683 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored
cargo test --lib: 341 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored (340 base + 1 new terminal_failure_clears_retry_tracking)
cargo clippy: no warnings or errors (Checking ralph-burning → Finished dev profile)
cargo fmt --check: no formatting differences (exit 0, no output)

resume_state_tests::terminal_failure_clears_retry_tracking: 4 retryable StageFailed(will_retry=true) at attempts 1-4, then StageFailed(will_retry=false) at attempt 5 → resume cursor at attempt=1 (not 6), confirming the SC-RESUME-005 fix
resume_state_tests::resume_after_retryable_failure_preserves_attempt: 3 retryable failures → resume at attempt 4 (unchanged, no regression)
resume_state_tests::completion_round_advance_resets_retry_tracking: round 1 failures don't carry into round 2 (unchanged, no regression)
Pre-backoff snapshot write at engine.rs:4643-4648 now uses ? propagation, ensuring journal/snapshot consistency when the stage_failed event has already been durably written
CycleAdvanced handler at engine.rs:5852 now calls .retain() to drop stale entries for prior cycles, matching CompletionRoundAdvanced's .clear() pattern

apply_jitter in retry_policy.rs now uses murmur-style hash mixing (h ^= h >> 16; h = h.wrapping_mul(0x45d9f3b); h ^= h >> 16) before modulo, improving jitter distribution on coarse-clock systems without adding dependencies

Existing jitter tests (jitter_enabled_backoff_stays_within_expected_range, jitter_never_exceeds_backoff_cap) continue to pass — the hash mixing preserves the [0.75, 1.25) output range and the post-jitter re-cap to backoff_cap
